title: Review hospital station add-versus-remove tote failure state
operational_intent: Establish a source-backed symptom baseline for hospital induction failure so later recovery actions can be compared against documented pre-recovery state.

steps.instruction: Open the Salesforce case intake/closure evidence and record the exact wording that the ACB hospital was unable to induct and that the robot was in shutdown state or unable to return to normal operation.
steps.validation_check: The captured case evidence explicitly ties the incident to hospital induction failure and robot shutdown/normal-state recovery language.

steps.instruction: Review the Teams troubleshooting thread and capture the message stating that the site can remove totes but cannot add totes at the hospital.
steps.validation_check: The symptom is preserved as an asymmetric hospital failure, not a generic tote handling outage.

steps.instruction: Capture the later Salesforce/Teams update stating the original issue was resolved and the site could add and remove totes again.
steps.validation_check: Recovery evidence explicitly confirms both hospital add and remove functions were restored.

validation_checks: Hospital symptom is documented as remove works but add fails, or inability to induct is explicitly recorded
Robot shutdown or inability to return to normal operation is preserved if visible in case evidence
escalation_conditions: Hospital symptom cannot be clearly confirmed
Robot remains in shutdown state after recovery attempts
Evidence suggests broader service or AGV state issue beyond hospital UI behavior
synthesis_blockers: Hospital UI path and field-level validation are incomplete
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful as a recurring CAT-1/CAT-3 crossover symptom review pattern where hospital remove works but add fails.

title: Review AGV misplacement and disconnected tote evidence around hospital blockage
operational_intent: Document the AGV state anomalies associated with hospital blockage so recovery actions can be traced to specific AGVs and tote associations.

steps.instruction: Review the Teams troubleshooting messages and record each AGV explicitly named in relation to the blockage, including AGV 1555028, AGV 1542188, and AGV 1555012 if referenced in the recovery summary.
steps.validation_check: Each AGV ID is tied to a visible source message rather than memory or inference.

steps.instruction: Capture the Teams statements that AGV 1555028 does not belong there, that two AGVs are under the rack in the same zone, and that AGV 1542188 is at the hospital pickup location.
steps.validation_check: The anomaly description is preserved exactly as source-provided and not upgraded to validated root cause.

steps.instruction: Open the robot details screenshot and record the visible high-confidence fields for robot 1555028, including Robot ID, Path Mode, confirmed location wording, node code, and any visible action buttons such as Remove or Restart.
steps.validation_check: Only high-confidence visible fields are recorded; noisy OCR-only fields are not over-trusted.

steps.instruction: Capture the recovery summary text stating that AGV 1555012 had disconnected tote 51 in the system and that tote 51 could not be removed from hospital until API dog was used.
steps.validation_check: The disconnected tote evidence is tied to a specific AGV and tote ID in source text.

validation_checks: Specific AGV IDs are preserved
Wrong-zone or under-rack observations are tied to source evidence
Disconnected tote reference is linked to the implicated AGV when visible
escalation_conditions: AGV identity cannot be confirmed
Multiple AGVs appear misplaced or under rack
Disconnected tote cannot be cleared after AGV cleanup
synthesis_blockers: Field-level AGV state validation path is incomplete
pattern_candidate_notes: This candidate may generalize to CAT-1/CAT-2 crossover incidents where hospital blockage is driven by AGV placement or stale AGV/tote state.

title: Document API dog or equivalent endpoint-based tote recovery actions
operational_intent: Preserve a reusable candidate recovery pattern for endpoint-based tote actions while clearly marking missing operational details and role-review requirements.

steps.instruction: Review the recovery summary and record the exact failed action, such as tote induction not working at the hospital or tote 51 not being removable from hospital.
steps.validation_check: The failed normal-path tote action is explicitly documented before the endpoint-based action is recorded.

steps.instruction: Capture the statement that a tote was inducted with API dog to get the system moving forward, and record that this was an endpoint-based action rather than a standard hospital UI action.
steps.validation_check: The source explicitly ties API dog tote induction to recovery progress.

steps.instruction: Capture the statement that AGV 1555012 had disconnected tote 51 and that API dog was used to remove tote 51 after it could not be removed from hospital.
steps.validation_check: The tote removal action is linked to a specific tote and AGV in source evidence.

steps.instruction: Review the follow-up Teams discussion and record that API dog process documentation was requested, that the process had been covered in training, and that Aveva was said to hit the same endpoints as API dog.
steps.validation_check: The procedure remains candidate-only and clearly marked as needing role and process validation.

validation_checks: Source evidence explicitly shows which tote action failed and which endpoint-based action was used
Post-action recovery is documented
escalation_conditions: Normal hospital removal path still unclear
Endpoint-based action is unavailable or unauthorized
Issue persists after tote induction/removal action
synthesis_blockers: Tooling details and permissions are incomplete
pattern_candidate_notes: Important candidate recovery pattern, but should remain review-only until endpoint actions are formally documented and approved.

title: Validate recovery after Optisweep service reset, hospital lane restart, and robot re-add
operational_intent: Create a reusable post-recovery validation sequence for incidents where service reset and hospital lane restart are documented but exact execution details are incomplete.

steps.instruction: Capture the documented recovery statement that the site is now able to add and remove totes, and record any note that secondary cleanup issues still remained at that moment.
steps.validation_check: Recovery evidence explicitly confirms restoration of both hospital add and remove functions.

steps.instruction: Capture the later update stating that all robots were re-added and are back up and running.
steps.validation_check: The source shows fleet-level recovery, not only partial hospital symptom relief.

steps.instruction: Review the Teams recovery summary and capture statements such as system started moving, clearing up hospital now, system is running now, or issue resolved around 9 PM ET.
steps.validation_check: At least one post-action message confirms operational movement or running state, not just action completion.

steps.instruction: Capture the closure wording that Optisweep services were reset and the site restarted Hospital Lane, then record the escalation condition that the robot does not return to normal operation after those actions.
steps.validation_check: Documented closure actions are preserved and can be compared against any recurrence or failed recovery.

validation_checks: Hospital can add and remove totes
Robots are re-added and running
System is moving or site is running
escalation_conditions: Robot does not return to normal operation
Hospital still cannot add totes
System is not moving after robot re-add
synthesis_blockers: Post-recovery validation method is partly narrative rather than screen-driven
pattern_candidate_notes: Good candidate for CAT-1 recovery validation where service reset and robot re-add are documented but root cause remains unvalidated.

title: Restart Ignition/OptiSweep service path and validate application response
operational_intent: Restore WCS/OptiSweep application responsiveness and confirm the service stack is responding before onsite operations resume.

steps.instruction: Confirm with onsite personnel that the system is in a safe state for remote recovery actions and that the system will remain e-stopped until remote validation is complete.
steps.validation_check: Onsite team confirms the system is held in e-stop and remote restart can proceed.

steps.instruction: On the affected server, open Command Prompt and navigate to the Ignition installation directory shown in the evidence before issuing the gateway restart command.
steps.validation_check: The command window shows the operator is in the Ignition directory and ready to execute the restart command.

steps.instruction: Run the gateway restart command visible in the case evidence and wait for the restart sequence to complete.
steps.validation_check: The command output indicates the gateway restart was initiated and no immediate command failure is shown.

steps.instruction: Open Services (Local), locate the OptiSweep service entry, and capture its visible status and startup type.
steps.validation_check: OptiSweep service is visible and shows Running after the restart sequence.

steps.instruction: Use the visible API test view to send the GetAgvStatuses request, or the equivalent health-check request used by support, and capture the response.
steps.validation_check: The API returns a response after restart, matching the guidance that service response should be confirmed before releasing e-stop.

steps.instruction: Inform onsite personnel that restart validation has passed and they may release the e-stop only after service response has been confirmed.
steps.validation_check: Onsite release instruction is given only after remote validation evidence is captured.

validation_checks: Ignition gateway restart command completes without visible error
OptiSweep service shows running state in Services view
API request returns a response after restart
Onsite team can release e-stop only after remote validation
escalation_conditions: Remote access unavailable
Gateway restart does not restore API response
OptiSweep service cannot be restarted or remains unstable
Issue recurs and infrastructure review is needed
synthesis_blockers: Needs SME confirmation of exact restart order and permissions
pattern_candidate_notes: Matches CAT-1 service failure pattern where AGVs stop moving and recovery depends on remote service restart plus post-restart validation.

title: Review AGV states and correct out-of-sync AGVs in RMS after service recovery
operational_intent: Restore AGV fleet consistency after service restart by reviewing AGV states in RMS and related status views, then correcting affected AGVs individually.

steps.instruction: Open Geek+ RMS and navigate to the robot map or monitor view shown in the evidence so individual robot details and current state can be reviewed.
steps.validation_check: RMS is accessible and at least one AGV detail record can be opened for inspection.

steps.instruction: Open the API test view and run the GetAgvStatuses request to capture returned AGV states that may indicate queue or shutdown mismatches.
steps.validation_check: The API returns current AGV state data that can be compared against RMS observations.

steps.instruction: Compare RMS robot details, API state output, and any visible system error notes to identify AGVs that are in incorrect queue states, missing from expected zone coverage, or otherwise inconsistent with normal operation.
steps.validation_check: A candidate list of AGVs requiring correction is documented before changes are made.

steps.instruction: Using RMS elevated controls, correct each AGV identified as out of sync one at a time, documenting the AGV ID and the action taken for each correction.
steps.validation_check: Each corrected AGV shows an improved or expected state before moving to the next AGV.

steps.instruction: After completing individual AGV corrections, rerun the AGV status review and confirm with onsite operations that the system can resume the intended process such as auto bag-out.
steps.validation_check: No critical AGV mismatch remains that blocks operation, and onsite team confirms recovery.

validation_checks: Affected AGVs can be identified in RMS or API views
Each corrected AGV no longer shows the problematic state
Zone missing AGV or queue anomalies no longer block operation
escalation_conditions: Cannot identify which AGVs are out of sync
Zone missing AGV signal persists after corrections
RMS access unavailable or insufficient
Multiple AGVs remain blocked after one-by-one correction
synthesis_blockers: Needs SME-approved AGV state interpretation and correction actions
pattern_candidate_notes: Fits CAT-1 incidents where service recovery alone is insufficient and AGVs remain out of sync until individually corrected in RMS.

title: Collect WCS server, application, and performance evidence for suspected timeout or crash condition
operational_intent: Capture the visible server, application, and event evidence needed to support later engineering or infrastructure review without over-claiming root cause.

steps.instruction: Record the reported fault time and the active diagnostic hypothesis from the case notes or Teams discussion, such as web application fault, memory concern, or possible crash/timeout.
steps.validation_check: A documented starting point exists for what evidence the team is trying to confirm or rule out.

steps.instruction: Open the Ignition status/performance page shown in the evidence and capture the visible CPU, memory, threads, and uptime-related information.
steps.validation_check: The screenshot clearly shows the performance page and enough metric context for later review.

steps.instruction: Capture the memory trend view and note the visible scale and time window so later reviewers can compare pre- and post-restart behavior.
steps.validation_check: The memory trend screenshot is readable enough to support later comparison, even if it does not prove causation.

steps.instruction: Open Windows Services and capture the OptiSweep service row, including status and startup type.
steps.validation_check: The service state is preserved as visible evidence for later comparison with reported outage behavior.

steps.instruction: If a restart is performed, capture the command prompt or server console showing the restart command and any resulting status text.
steps.validation_check: The evidence package includes a visible record of the restart attempt rather than only a verbal report.

steps.instruction: If Windows Event Viewer, Ignition logs, or application event screens are available during the session, capture the relevant entries around the reported fault time; if they are not visible in the case, explicitly note that event evidence was not captured from this package.
steps.validation_check: Either timestamped event evidence is captured, or the evidence gap is explicitly documented for escalation.

validation_checks: Performance page shows CPU/memory/threads or uptime data
Service state is captured from Windows Services
Any restart command or server console evidence is preserved
If event logs are available, relevant application/system entries are captured with timestamps
escalation_conditions: No event evidence can be collected before restart
Service crashes repeatedly
Performance evidence suggests infrastructure follow-up is needed
Visible evidence is insufficient to distinguish timeout from memory issue
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful as a reusable evidence-collection pattern for CAT-1 incidents where memory, timeout, or crash is suspected but not validated.

title: Establish customer bridge and onsite coordination before CAT-1 remote recovery actions
operational_intent: Ensure remote troubleshooting and recovery actions occur with active onsite coordination and shared visibility into system state.

steps.instruction: Ask whether a customer-facing bridge is active and whether onsite UPS contacts have been invited before continuing remote recovery planning.
steps.validation_check: The incident lead can state whether a real customer bridge exists and who is on it.

steps.instruction: If no proper bridge exists, start a group call or meeting immediately and add the onsite UPS contacts identified in the incident thread.
steps.validation_check: At least one onsite decision-maker is on the bridge or has confirmed receipt of the invite.

steps.instruction: Ask the onsite team to state whether they are manually bagging out, whether the system is e-stopped, and when they will be ready for remote recovery actions.
steps.validation_check: Remote team has a current, explicit statement of onsite system state before acting.

steps.instruction: Do not proceed with remote restart or validation steps without keeping the onsite team on the bridge for status updates, release timing, and confirmation of operational recovery.
steps.validation_check: Onsite team remains informed during remote actions and confirms recovery status after validation.

validation_checks: A customer bridge is active with onsite participants
Onsite contact names and invite status are documented
Bag-out and e-stop status are confirmed before remote actions
escalation_conditions: No onsite contact joins the bridge
Customer reports not receiving updates or invite
Remote recovery is blocked because site state is unclear
pattern_candidate_notes: Strong candidate for CAT-1 incident handling because missing bridge materially affected coordination in this case.

title: Check tipper heartbeat state and local tipper response during CAT-1 no-movement incident
operational_intent: Collect source-backed tipper heartbeat and local-state evidence that distinguishes a no-alarm AGV stop from a tipper heartbeat/comms issue.

steps.instruction: Ask the site to open the operator-facing RMS/related local view and report whether AGVs are stopping before tipper processing while beacon lights remain green and no active operator-station alarms are visible.
steps.validation_check: Evidence shows the system is stopped without a clear local alarm explanation.

steps.instruction: Have the site open AVEVA or the equivalent tipper overview and compare displayed tipper state to what is physically observed in the field, specifically whether tippers appear closed on screen while physically open and not paused.
steps.validation_check: A documented mismatch exists between AVEVA/supervisory state and field state, or the screen confirms no mismatch.

steps.instruction: Direct the site to attempt a pause and then unpause of an affected tipper from the HMI, and record whether the command executes or has no effect.
steps.validation_check: The result is explicitly recorded as successful, unsuccessful, or indeterminate.

steps.instruction: On the tipper HMI, read and capture the visible heartbeat values for each tipper, preserving the full set of values and identifying any tipper showing zero or timeout behavior.
steps.validation_check: At least one screenshot or written record preserves the heartbeat values, including any zero value such as Tipper 1 = 0.

steps.instruction: Summarize the collected evidence in the case or bridge chat, including no-alarm condition, AVEVA mismatch if present, pause/unpause result, and heartbeat values, and flag whether engineer restart or controls escalation is needed.
steps.validation_check: The next responder can see the symptom pattern and supporting values without re-running the checks.

validation_checks: Heartbeat values are captured and at least one abnormal value or timeout condition is documented.
Pause/unpause result is documented as successful or unsuccessful.
AVEVA/local field-state mismatch is documented if visible.
escalation_conditions: All tippers show heartbeat timeout or one/more heartbeat values are zero/non-updating.
Pause/unpause does not restore response.
Hardware alarms are visible on AVEVA/RMS/controls screens.
Support cannot obtain remote access for deeper review.
synthesis_blockers: Need SME confirmation of screen names and role boundaries.
pattern_candidate_notes: Matches CAT-1 recurrence pattern in related cases 229716 and 229777 where heartbeat failure and no visible RMS faults were key entry symptoms.

title: Restore access and perform engineer-led Optisweep service restart for CAT-1 WCS service failure
operational_intent: Recover CAT-1 service-failure incidents by restoring server access and executing the source-backed service restart path.

steps.instruction: Record that normal UPS-side ZScaler/VPN access is failing after authentication and note any related UPS incident or helpline information before switching to the field-PC access path.
steps.validation_check: The team has documented that normal remote access is blocked and an alternate path is required.

steps.instruction: Confirm the primary WCS IP to target for access, using the incident thread evidence that identifies 10.27.80.165 as the primary WCS server needed to check Ignition and the database.
steps.validation_check: The responder can state which server is being accessed and why.

steps.instruction: Coordinate with the site to use the shared field PC and establish an RDP session to 10.27.80.165, then confirm that the remote desktop session is active.
steps.validation_check: The team can interact with the primary WCS server through the field-PC session.

steps.instruction: Once server access is available, engage the engineer or elevated responder who has authority to use administrative access and perform the restart, and record the responder name in the case.
steps.validation_check: An authorized responder is clearly identified before the restart occurs.

steps.instruction: From the server session, have the authorized responder restart the Optisweep services used by the WCS/Ignition environment, and document that the restart was completed.
steps.validation_check: The case or bridge explicitly states that Optisweep services were restarted.

steps.instruction: Ask the site and bridge participants to confirm whether the system is operational after the restart and whether the site continues to run, then record the outcome in the case.
steps.validation_check: At least one post-restart note confirms operational recovery from the production perspective.

validation_checks: RDP session to 10.27.80.165 is established.
Engineer confirms Optisweep services were restarted.
Post-restart system operation is confirmed by site or case notes.
escalation_conditions: RDP path cannot be established.
Restart does not restore operation.
Heartbeat or hardware alarms persist after restart.
Issue recurs in the same sort window.
synthesis_blockers: Need exact service inventory and approved access method.
pattern_candidate_notes: Strong recurrence candidate across CAT-1 cases where service restart restored operation after heartbeat/no-alarm symptoms.

title: Collect WCS server and application evidence for database timeout symptoms
operational_intent: Create a reusable evidence-collection pattern for database timeout symptoms seen during CAT-1 incidents without over-interpreting them as root cause.

steps.instruction: Use the available server access path or existing RCA screenshots to open the WCS/Ignition-side evidence source that contains WCSWebApplication timeout entries.
steps.validation_check: The reviewer is looking at the event source that contains the timeout evidence, not a paraphrased note only.

steps.instruction: Capture the event list or screenshot that shows multiple WCSWebApplication errors at the same timestamp, including the visible 4/4/2026 1:48:37 AM entries and event IDs if shown.
steps.validation_check: The screenshot clearly preserves the clustered event timing and source attribution.

steps.instruction: Open a WCSWebApplication Event ID 20102 entry and capture the General Details pane showing the category, event ID, failed DbCommand text, command timeout, and logged time.
steps.validation_check: The captured detail pane contains enough text to identify this as a database-command timeout event.

steps.instruction: Open a WCSWebApplication Event ID 10100 entry and capture the General Details pane showing the query exception, context type, and Execution Timeout Expired wording.
steps.validation_check: The captured detail pane clearly ties the event to a query timeout/iteration exception.

steps.instruction: Record the visible host/computer name and correlate the timeout timestamps to the incident timeline, noting that the screenshots were later shared as database timeouts around 2 AM site time.
steps.validation_check: A reviewer can connect the event screenshots to the incident timeline without re-reading the entire chat thread.

validation_checks: At least one event 10100 and/or 20102 record is captured with timestamp and source.
Host/computer name is preserved if visible.
Event details include timeout wording rather than only a generic error count.
escalation_conditions: Timeout events are present near the incident window.
Timeouts recur after restart.
Timeout evidence is accompanied by broader server/database health concerns.
synthesis_blockers: Need validated event collection method from a live server example.
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful as a reusable evidence-collection object because database timeout screenshots recur in CAT-1 investigations even when they are not the validated root cause.

title: Collect and correlate OPC or tipper endpoint communication evidence
operational_intent: Preserve communication-path evidence for engineering and controls review without overclaiming causality.

steps.instruction: Capture the visible connectivity test output for the tipper OTnet endpoint, preserving the target IP 10.27.80.157, timestamps, and response times shown in the PowerShell or command prompt output.
steps.validation_check: The screenshot clearly ties the connectivity evidence to endpoint 10.27.80.157.

steps.instruction: Open the OPC or Ignition log view that shows UascClientMessageHandler entries and capture the rows showing connection reset events to remote 10.27.80.157:4840.
steps.validation_check: At least one screenshot preserves a connection-reset event to the target endpoint.

steps.instruction: Capture the log or screenshot entries showing WriteOpcTagAsync failures, Bad at OPC comms errors, and any visible heartbeat or hospital tag names affected.
steps.validation_check: The evidence shows that operational tag writes were failing, not just generic connectivity warnings.

steps.instruction: Capture the alarm screen showing any tipper or controls hardware alarms, preserving timestamps and exact alarm text such as RIO Module Ethernet Failure, ClearLink Module not communicating, and VFD not detected.
steps.validation_check: Alarm evidence is preserved with exact wording and timestamps for later controls review.

steps.instruction: Create a concise evidence note that links the endpoint IP, connection-reset entries, OPC write failures, and any controls alarms, while explicitly marking them as candidate contributing evidence rather than validated root cause.
steps.validation_check: A reviewer can see the communication-path evidence set without inferring that causality is already proven.

validation_checks: Endpoint IP 10.27.80.157 is preserved in the evidence.
At least one OPC/UASC connection-reset or Bad at OPC comms entry is captured.
Any related controls alarms are preserved with timestamps if visible.
escalation_conditions: Connection reset or Bad at OPC comms evidence is present.
Controls alarms are visible.
Heartbeat does not recover after service restart or issue recurs.
synthesis_blockers: Need validated live collection steps and controls review boundaries.
pattern_candidate_notes: Strong candidate for recurrence grouping where heartbeat failure coincides with OPC connection resets and OT-side alarm evidence.

title: Restart Optisweep Windows service after AGVs stop with no visible fault
operational_intent: Restore AGV movement and production when the Optisweep Windows service is suspected stopped or unresponsive.

steps.instruction: Review the active Teams thread and Salesforce case to confirm the reported symptom is AGVs not moving, no visible fault is reported, and customer/site is requesting rapid recovery.
steps.validation_check: Evidence shows AGVs are not moving and the issue is being handled as a CAT-1 service failure candidate rather than a confirmed hardware fault.

steps.instruction: Ask site/support to confirm current tipper/HMI status and record the reported findings that tippers were mostly in run position, heartbeat was flat at 550, and no HMI system-level errors were reported.
steps.validation_check: Pre-restart evidence supports a no-visible-fault presentation consistent with service restart being considered.

steps.instruction: Coordinate with site/support in the incident thread to confirm the area is stopped or startup is being held before the remote responder restarts the Optisweep Windows service.
steps.validation_check: There is visible coordination showing restart was intentionally executed during a controlled recovery attempt.

steps.instruction: From the remote site connection, restart the Optisweep Windows service and immediately notify the incident thread that the restart has been completed.
steps.validation_check: A support message or case comment explicitly confirms the Optisweep Windows service restart occurred.

steps.instruction: Monitor site feedback and case comments for startup progress and production confirmation after the service restart.
steps.validation_check: Recovery is only considered successful when startup and production statements are explicitly documented.

steps.instruction: Update the case to reflect recovery, resolution/RCA-pending status, and note that logs or additional evidence are still being collected for investigation.
steps.validation_check: Case record shows recovery was documented without claiming validated root cause.

validation_checks: Site startup begins after restart
Support or site confirms production resumes
Case comments reflect recovery and RCA continuation
escalation_conditions: AGVs remain stopped after restart
Active hardware alarms are present
Remote access is unavailable
Issue recurs within same shift or same pattern as prior case
synthesis_blockers: Missing exact service UI path and host details
pattern_candidate_notes: Strongly aligns with CAT-1 recurrence pattern where AGVs stop with no visible fault and restart restores production.

title: Collect Ignition OPC UA disconnect and related alarm evidence for recurrence investigation
operational_intent: Preserve the visible WCS/application/event evidence needed to investigate communication resets and related controls alarms without inventing unsupported navigation or database details.

steps.instruction: Capture the Teams messages that state the site is having the same issue again as Saturday case 229374 and that RCA/root-cause information is being requested.
steps.validation_check: The evidence package clearly ties the captured diagnostics to a recurrence investigation rather than an isolated one-off review.

steps.instruction: Open the Ignition diagnostics log viewer page visible in the source and capture screenshots that show logger name, timestamp, remote endpoint IP/port, and the exact error text such as 'Exception caught: Connection reset'.
steps.validation_check: At least one screenshot clearly shows a logger, timestamp, endpoint, and reset/error message together on the same screen.

steps.instruction: From the same Ignition diagnostics/log viewer area, capture entries showing keep-alive failures, subscription transfer failures, Bad_ServiceUnsupported, reconnect attempts, resend success, and monitored item rebuild activity.
steps.validation_check: Screenshots preserve both failure and recovery/retry messages so later reviewers can assess whether reconnect logic partially recovered.

steps.instruction: Capture the alarm/history screens that show the prior-night fault window and preserve the exact alarm text, timestamps, status, and affected tipper side/device references.
steps.validation_check: Alarm screenshots show exact message text and timestamps sufficient to correlate with the investigated fault window.

steps.instruction: Capture the endpoint status page and heartbeat statistics screen showing which endpoints are connected or disabled and the visible heartbeat values after recovery or manual reset.
steps.validation_check: Screenshots show whether communications were reestablished and which endpoints remained disabled or recently reset.

steps.instruction: Capture the Teams discussion that references DB event logs showing timeouts, and record that this case supports only a visible discussion reference rather than a direct database log artifact. If available in future cases, pair this with the actual WCS server/application/event screenshot or export.
steps.validation_check: The evidence package clearly distinguishes between a discussion reference to DB timeouts and an actual captured DB log/event artifact.

steps.instruction: Capture the disconnect count chart and follow-up Teams messages that clarify the team is tracking station disconnects and preparing daily reports for the customer.
steps.validation_check: Monitoring evidence shows recurrence tracking continued after operational recovery.

validation_checks: Captured screenshots show logger names, timestamps, remote endpoint IPs, and error strings
Alarm evidence preserves exact message text and timestamps
Endpoint status evidence shows connected/disabled state at time of review
escalation_conditions: Ignition diagnostics are inaccessible
Endpoint IP ownership cannot be mapped
Controls alarms indicate OT-side device failure
Evidence suggests recurring disconnect pattern requiring engineering/controls review
synthesis_blockers: Missing direct export artifacts and DB evidence
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful reusable evidence-collection pattern for CAT-1 recurrence investigations involving OPC UA disconnects and controls alarms.

title: Validate post-restart recovery and document RCA-pending status
operational_intent: Ensure restart-based recovery is explicitly validated and documented without overstating root cause closure.

steps.instruction: Review the post-restart Teams and Salesforce updates and capture the first statements indicating the site is about to try startup or is starting up.
steps.validation_check: There is explicit evidence that startup was attempted after the restart, not merely that the restart command was issued.

steps.instruction: Record the first explicit statement that the site is operating and getting production, and preserve the associated timestamp and source.
steps.validation_check: Recovery is validated only when production restoration is explicitly stated.

steps.instruction: Update or capture the Salesforce status history showing the case moved from escalated to resolved and/or RCA pending, and preserve any note that information is still being pulled for RCA.
steps.validation_check: Case documentation shows recovery closure and ongoing investigation as separate states.

steps.instruction: Review post-recovery Teams discussion for residual issues such as tote removal problems, shutdown-before-bagout state, or parcel/bag-tag concerns, and record them as follow-up operational risks.
steps.validation_check: Residual issues are documented as separate follow-up items and do not overwrite the fact that production recovery occurred.

steps.instruction: If the case moves into monitoring, capture the next-day disconnect discussion and any chart/report requests showing ongoing observation of station disconnects or similar recurrence indicators.
steps.validation_check: Monitoring evidence shows the incident transitioned from active recovery to observation rather than immediate full RCA closure.

validation_checks: Startup is attempted and succeeds
Production is explicitly reported as restored
Residual issues are captured separately from the main outage recovery
Case status reflects resolved/RCA pending rather than validated root cause
escalation_conditions: Production does not resume after restart
Residual tote/hospital issues persist
Repeated disconnects continue after recovery
Customer requests RCA or monitoring data beyond support's current evidence
synthesis_blockers: Residual issue handling not fully specified
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful for restart-based CAT-1 incidents where production returns quickly but RCA and monitoring continue afterward.

title: Review tipper heartbeat timeout state and RMS no-fault condition
operational_intent: Preserve the observable pre-restart failure pattern so support and engineering can compare recurrence against prior heartbeat timeout incidents.

steps.instruction: Record the reported operational condition that sort started normally, then all three lines stopped as AGVs lined up to go to the tippers, and note that nothing was coming to the tippers.
steps.validation_check: The captured narrative explicitly ties the stop to AGVs lining up for tippers and not a generic site-down report.

steps.instruction: Open or preserve the visible tipper heartbeat/alarm screen and capture the screen showing heartbeat timeout indications for the tippers.
steps.validation_check: The screenshot set shows heartbeat timeout or heartbeat-related stale condition on the tipper view and can be linked to the same incident window.

steps.instruction: Capture the RMS screen or note from the source showing that no faults were visible while the tippers were timing out and AGV flow was stopped.
steps.validation_check: Evidence shows the no-fault RMS condition in the same incident window as the heartbeat timeout report.

steps.instruction: Record the observed combination of all-three-line stop, heartbeat timeout, active/enabled tipper state, no RMS faults, and hospital tote removal failure in the case notes or evidence package.
steps.validation_check: The evidence package contains enough detail to distinguish this pattern from generic AGV stop or hardware-fault incidents.

validation_checks: Heartbeat timeout is visibly shown for all tippers or affected tippers
Tipper status appears active/enabled in the same evidence set
RMS screen shows no visible faults at the same time window
escalation_conditions: Heartbeat view cannot be accessed remotely
Hardware alarms are visible instead of no-fault pattern
Heartbeat does not recover after restart
Observed pattern differs materially from prior CAT-1 recurrence
synthesis_blockers: Visual evidence quality limits exact field extraction
pattern_candidate_notes: Strong match to CAT-1 recurrence pattern described in dataset context for cases 229374 and 229777.

title: Coordinate AGV E-stop and Optisweep service restart for CAT-1 heartbeat timeout pattern
operational_intent: Restore AGV and hospital operation after the documented heartbeat timeout/service failure pattern while preserving role boundaries and evidence.

steps.instruction: Review the live support thread and confirm the proposed recovery sequence is to E-stop AGVs, restart the service, and remove the E-stop, with Optisweep identified as the service to restart.
steps.validation_check: The support thread explicitly identifies Optisweep as the restart target and not an assumed generic service reset.

steps.instruction: Have the site operator place the AGVs or system into E-stop as directed in the support thread and document who confirmed the E-stop was applied.
steps.validation_check: There is explicit confirmation in the incident record that the system was E-stopped before or during the restart sequence.

steps.instruction: From the remote Windows service control view or equivalent host access, restart the Optisweep service identified in the support thread.
steps.validation_check: The restart action is documented and tied to the Optisweep service rather than an unspecified application restart.

steps.instruction: Coordinate with the site to remove the E-stop after the Optisweep restart and document the time and confirmation source.
steps.validation_check: The incident record shows E-stop removal as part of the executed recovery sequence, not just a proposed step.

steps.instruction: Check for post-restart evidence that AGVs are moving and that hospital tote removal is working again; if hospital removal still fails, perform the documented hospital log out/in and recheck.
steps.validation_check: Recovery is only marked successful when both AGV movement and hospital function are confirmed.

validation_checks: E-stop was applied before restart if required by site practice
Optisweep was the selected restart target
AGVs resume movement after restart and E-stop removal
Hospital function is restored
escalation_conditions: Remote access unavailable
Heartbeat does not recover after restart
Hardware alarms are present
Issue recurs within same shift or sort window
synthesis_blockers: Some execution details remain role- and site-specific
pattern_candidate_notes: Strongly aligned with CAT-1 resolution approach in dataset context: service restart with E-stop coordination.

title: Validate AGV movement and hospital tote removal after CAT-1 service recovery
operational_intent: Confirm true operational recovery and avoid closing the incident based only on restart completion.

steps.instruction: Ask the remote observer or site contact to verify AGVs are moving and capture the confirmation in the support thread or case notes.
steps.validation_check: AGV movement is explicitly confirmed after the restart sequence, not inferred from service status alone.

steps.instruction: Have the site retest tote removal at the hospital station and document whether the remove command now executes.
steps.validation_check: The same operational function that failed earlier is explicitly confirmed as restored.

steps.instruction: If the hospital still cannot remove totes after the service restart, have the local user log out and back in at the hospital station, then retest tote removal.
steps.validation_check: Hospital tote removal succeeds after the logout/login if it did not recover immediately after restart.

steps.instruction: Record the final confirmation from the site contact that AGV operation and hospital function are proper, then note that the system is operational and the call can disengage.
steps.validation_check: Closure-ready validation includes both internal support confirmation and site/customer confirmation where available.

validation_checks: AGVs are moving
Hospital tote removal works
System can be declared operational
Customer/site contact confirms proper operation
escalation_conditions: AGVs move but hospital tote removal still fails after logout/login
System appears operational but customer cannot confirm production function
Issue recurs shortly after validation
synthesis_blockers: Hospital workaround may not generalize without SME review
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful as a reusable post-restart validation object because this case shows restart completion alone was not the full validation boundary.

title: Collect server event, Ignition, and requested DB timeout evidence after CAT-1 recovery
operational_intent: Preserve post-incident evidence for engineering review without inventing unsupported database collection steps.

steps.instruction: Capture the support instruction that event logs and DB logs should be collected, and note any additional request for performance monitor or switch-log follow-up.
steps.validation_check: The evidence package clearly distinguishes requested evidence from evidence actually collected.

steps.instruction: From the remote Windows host, save or export the Windows event logs referenced in the case, and capture the location where the files were stored if the export itself is not shown.
steps.validation_check: There is explicit evidence that Windows/server event logs were saved or downloaded, even if the exact export UI is not shown.

steps.instruction: Save the Ignition logs referenced in the support thread and capture any visible filename fragment, save location, or related performance-monitor screenshot request tied to Ignition.
steps.validation_check: Ignition evidence is preserved with at least a saved-file reference and location, even if the full file path is not shown.

steps.instruction: Check whether any DB logs, DB timeout messages, or database-related application evidence are visibly available from the current remote session or saved files; if not, document that DB logs were requested but the collector was not familiar with the method to obtain or transfer them.
steps.validation_check: The case record clearly distinguishes between DB evidence requested and DB evidence actually collected; if unavailable, the gap is explicitly documented.

steps.instruction: Record which logs were saved or downloaded, where they were staged, whether they were attached to the ticket, and what remained outstanding such as DB logs or switch logs.
steps.validation_check: The final evidence record states both what was collected and what still requires follow-up, avoiding false completeness.

validation_checks: Ignition logs are saved or exported
Windows event logs are saved or exported
Case notes state whether DB logs were collected or remain a gap
Evidence location is recorded for later transfer
escalation_conditions: DB timeout evidence cannot be located
No access to event logs or Ignition logs
Need DBA/infrastructure help to obtain DB logs
Switch or OT-side logs are also required
synthesis_blockers: Database evidence collection is incomplete and not operationally validated
Critical navigation and tool details are missing
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful as a bounded evidence-collection candidate because the case explicitly requests logs and explicitly documents the DB log gap.

title: Coordinate master E-stop with remote Optisweep restart for startup stall and tipper heartbeat recurrence
operational_intent: Restore system movement to tippers when startup is stalled and the incident matches the recurring tipper heartbeat failure pattern.

steps.instruction: Review the active case description and support chat, confirm the reported condition is startup with everything waiting, no path shown, and nothing reaching the tippers, and verify an engineer is actively connected or assigned to investigate.
steps.validation_check: Symptoms and escalation context match the recurring CAT-1 startup stall pattern and an engineer is available to perform remote recovery.

steps.instruction: Contact the site/client and instruct them to place the master E-stop on, then document that the site action was completed before or during the remote restart sequence.
steps.validation_check: Site confirms master E-stop is on before proceeding with or while coordinating the restart.

steps.instruction: From the remote Optisweep/WCS environment, perform the Optisweep restart used by the engineer during the incident and document that the restart was executed as part of the recovery sequence.
steps.validation_check: Restart action is completed and documented before tipper pause/unpause validation steps.

steps.instruction: Using the engineer-access tipper control interface, pause the tippers and then unpause them, matching the sequence documented in Teams and Salesforce after the Optisweep restart.
steps.validation_check: Tipper pause/unpause is completed after the restart and before final recovery confirmation.

steps.instruction: Verify that the system is back up and running, confirm that everything came back on, communicate with the customer, and document customer acceptance for closure if operations are restored.
steps.validation_check: Recovery is confirmed by both support and customer-facing evidence, and the case can be moved to resolved/closed state.

validation_checks: Engineer confirms remote connection established.
Recovery actions are completed in sequence.
System returns to back up and running state.
escalation_conditions: Heartbeat does not recover after restart.
Hardware alarms are present.
Remote access is unavailable.
Issue recurs within same shift or sort window.
synthesis_blockers: UI navigation details are not visible in this case package
pattern_candidate_notes: Strong recurrence alignment with cases 229716 and 229374 for startup stall plus tipper heartbeat failure pattern.

title: Review and document failed tipper heartbeat recurrence before recovery
operational_intent: Capture and preserve the recurrence-based diagnostic basis that the engineer used to guide recovery in case 229777.

steps.instruction: Open the Salesforce case description and capture the exact reported symptoms indicating startup stall, including everything waiting, no path shown, and nothing reaching the tippers.
steps.validation_check: The documented symptoms match the known recurrence entry pattern for this CAT-1 issue family.

steps.instruction: Review the Teams thread and capture the message where the engineer states they are connected and checking the system.
steps.validation_check: An engineer explicitly confirms remote connection before the diagnostic conclusion is recorded.

steps.instruction: From the Salesforce resolution note, capture the statement that the engineer dialed into the system and saw that the tipper heartbeat had failed.
steps.validation_check: The diagnostic note explicitly identifies failed tipper heartbeat as an observed condition, not just an inferred cause.

steps.instruction: Capture the same Salesforce resolution note text that states the failed tipper heartbeat was the same as case 229716, and record the prior case reference as recurrence context only.
steps.validation_check: The prior case reference is preserved as recurrence evidence and not promoted to validated root cause.

steps.instruction: Document that the heartbeat failure pattern was used as the recovery basis, then note missing direct evidence such as heartbeat screen capture, logs, or OT-side alarm review for this specific incident.
steps.validation_check: Observed signals are preserved while unresolved technical details remain explicitly open.

validation_checks: Current symptoms align with startup stall/no path/nothing reaching tippers.
Engineer documents that tipper heartbeat failed.
Prior comparable case reference is preserved without overclaiming root cause.
escalation_conditions: Heartbeat failure cannot be confirmed remotely.
Symptoms diverge from the prior recurrence pattern.
Hardware alarms or OT-side issues are visible and suggest a different failure mode.
synthesis_blockers: Need a repeatable heartbeat evidence capture standard
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful as a reusable recurrence-diagnosis object for CAT-1 startup stall incidents tied to tipper heartbeat failure language.

title: Collect WCS server, application, and event evidence for timeout or disconnect pattern review
operational_intent: Preserve the evidence collection actions that are visible in source and identify what should be captured from WCS/server/application/event views without inventing unsupported navigation details.

steps.instruction: Record the incident symptoms and the engineer's diagnostic statement that the tipper heartbeat failed in the same pattern as case 229716 before collecting deeper evidence.
steps.validation_check: The evidence package is anchored to a specific recurring failure pattern rather than a generic log pull.

steps.instruction: From the WCS or application server, open the server event view used by engineering and capture any events around the incident window that reference service faults, application faults, communication loss, timeout conditions, or restart activity. Preserve the exact event text rather than paraphrasing.
steps.validation_check: Captured event evidence includes exact timestamps and raw event text tied to the incident window.

steps.instruction: Open the WCS/Ignition application log source used by engineering and capture entries around the incident window that show heartbeat failures, communication loss, timeout messages, application faults, or restart sequences. Preserve exact strings and timestamps.
steps.validation_check: Application evidence includes exact raw strings and timestamps, not only a summary statement.

steps.instruction: If the issue appears to involve repeated disconnects or communication instability, collect the RMS disconnect logs or Athena logs referenced in the Teams follow-up and preserve which bots or components were affected.
steps.validation_check: Disconnect evidence identifies which bots/endpoints were affected and when.

steps.instruction: If onsite or engineering support sets up AGV-side logging, record which bots were instrumented and capture references to bot-side and xPico logs mentioned in the Teams follow-up.
steps.validation_check: Instrumentation details are specific enough for engineering to correlate bot-side evidence with server/application logs.

steps.instruction: Compile the collected evidence into one review bundle aligned to the incident timeline, preserving raw timestamps, source systems, event IDs, bot IDs, IPs, and exact timeout/disconnect/heartbeat wording for manual engineering review.
steps.validation_check: The final package allows engineering to compare incident timing against server/application/disconnect evidence without relying on memory or paraphrase.

validation_checks: Collected evidence includes timestamps and source system names.
Evidence set distinguishes server/application/event logs from chat commentary.
Any timeout or disconnect strings are preserved exactly as seen.
escalation_conditions: Required logs cannot be accessed remotely.
Issue recurs after restart-based recovery.
Disconnect or timeout evidence suggests OT/network/infrastructure involvement.
synthesis_blockers: Insufficient direct UI evidence for log collection navigation
Uncertain linkage of later follow-up logging discussion to the April 18 incident
pattern_candidate_notes: Useful as a review-only evidence collection scaffold for recurring CAT-1 timeout/disconnect investigations, but not ready for promotion without SME validation.

title: Manage callback, ownership, and resolution updates during CAT-1 recurrence
operational_intent: Reduce customer wait time and preserve clear ownership and closure communication during active incident handling.

steps.instruction: Post or capture a support-thread update that the same issue has happened again, include the approximate occurrence time if known, and request that UPS be called back with a follow-up update when resolved.
steps.validation_check: The support team can see that the issue recurred and that customer callback is required.

steps.instruction: If the customer is still waiting for a callback, post or capture a follow-up message asking whether the investigating person is in the group and identify the active owner.
steps.validation_check: An active investigator is identified or ownership escalation is visible in the thread.

steps.instruction: Capture the engineer message confirming remote connection and checking, and use that as the active ownership marker for the incident thread and case handling.
steps.validation_check: The thread clearly shows who is actively investigating the incident.

steps.instruction: After the engineer completes recovery, post or capture the update that the system is back up and running and include the key actions taken if known, such as restarting Optisweep and pausing/unpausing the tippers.
steps.validation_check: The support thread contains a clear recovery update tied to the incident.

steps.instruction: Update the Salesforce case with the recovery outcome, note that everything came back on and the client was happy for the case to be closed, then move the case to resolved/closed status.
steps.validation_check: Formal case record reflects recovery and customer-approved closure.

validation_checks: Customer callback need is acknowledged.
An active investigator is identified.
Recovery and closure updates are posted after resolution.
escalation_conditions: No engineer is assigned or responding.
Customer remains waiting without callback.
Issue recurs again after closure.
synthesis_blockers: Need standardized communication template for broader reuse
pattern_candidate_notes: This coordination pattern is reusable for CAT-1 incidents where customer callback pressure and investigator visibility are part of the escalation path.
